title: PM Lane 184 - Approval Persistence Readiness Grouping
authority: repo technical authority; PM lane implementation authority; final integration authority
objective: Reduce Project Miner Temp Power approval-readiness scan burden by grouping the existing Approval Persistence Readiness gates without changing gate ids, titles, details, status logic, readiness counts, readback card, route anchors, export references, disclosure behavior, no-storage posture, or approval/import write boundaries.
scope: Grouped the existing six Approval Persistence Readiness gates into Local Review Context, Hosted Persistence Surface, and Blocked Future Write Authority.
Preserved the Approval Status Readback card as non-gate context above the grouped readiness gates.

Preserved the same gate ids, titles, details, status logic, 4-of-6 readiness count, details disclosure behavior, no-disclosure-storage posture, route and quick-jump anchors, read seams, export references, and zero-mutation boundary.
Used a read-only sidecar to verify the grouping shape while VS Code Codex retained PM implementation and final integration authority.
guardrails: No Supabase, Render, Vercel, or Olares product action.
No browser approval button, approval POST wiring, approval submission, or approval-row creation.

No field authorization, assignment, schedule/status, durable field record, production tracking, customer report, completion evidence, billing, payroll, invoice, accounting, or external finance-system write.
No new export action, export handler, filename, payload version, localStorage key, backend route, service/auth/ingress change, or autonomous AI business-state mutation.
